message:
tools: add unit tests for proxy.go pure parsing functions (bd-7bown)

Cover proxyCompatible, jsonBoolField, jsonIntField, jsonStringField,
parseProxyRoutes, parseProxyFailoverEvents, and edge cases for
parseProxyStatusOutput and parseProxyStatusText. Tools package
coverage 55.0% to 55.9%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -234,7 +234,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1syw","title":"Task: Register agents with Agent Mail on spawn","description":"## Purpose
Automatically register each spawned agent with Agent Mail for inter-agent communication.

## Scope
- On spawn, call register_agent for each non-user pane
- Use auto-generated adjective+noun names per Agent Mail conventions
- Store pane -> agent name mapping for session
- Create project-wide coordination thread

## Registration Flow
for _, pane := range panes {
    if pane.Type == AgentUser { continue }
    
    agent, err := am.RegisterAgent(ctx, RegisterAgentOptions{
        ProjectKey:      s.projectDir,
        Program:         'ntm',
        Model:           pane.Variant,
        TaskDescription: fmt.Sprintf('Agent in pane %s', pane.Title),
    })
    
    s.agentRegistry[pane.ID] = agent.Name
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents registered with unique names
- Mapping stored for message routing
- Graceful handling when Agent Mail unavailable
- Registration survives session restart

## Technical Notes
Reuse existing agentmail client
Store mapping in session state","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1t881","title":"Test: Unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for LimitDetector pattern matching against actual agent output, covering all rate limit variations.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/limit_detector_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestLimitDetector_PatternMatching(t *testing.T) {
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    
    // Test all known rate limit patterns
    patterns := []struct {
        name       string
        agentType  string
        output     string
        shouldMatch bool
        matchType   string
    }{
        // Claude Code patterns
        {
            name:       "claude_rate_limit_standard",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Error: rate limit exceeded. Please wait before trying again.",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_429_error",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "API Error 429: Too Many Requests",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "429",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_token_limit",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "You have exceeded your token limit for this billing period",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "token_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "claude_normal_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "I'll help you implement that function. Let me start by...",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        
        // Codex patterns
        {
            name:       "codex_rate_limit",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "openai.RateLimitError: Rate limit reached for default-gpt-4",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "codex_quota_exceeded",
            agentType:  "cod",
            output:     "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Gemini patterns  
        {
            name:       "gemini_resource_exhausted",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "google.api_core.exceptions.ResourceExhausted: 429 Resource exhausted",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "resource_exhausted",
        },
        {
            name:       "gemini_quota_limit",
            agentType:  "gmi",
            output:     "Quota exceeded for GenerateContent",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "quota",
        },
        
        // Edge cases
        {
            name:       "partial_match",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "The word rate appears but limit does not follow immediately",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
        {
            name:       "case_insensitive",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "RATE LIMIT EXCEEDED",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "multiline_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "Processing...\nWorking...\nError: rate limit exceeded\nRetrying...",
            shouldMatch: true,
            matchType:   "rate_limit",
        },
        {
            name:       "empty_output",
            agentType:  "cc",
            output:     "",
            shouldMatch: false,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range patterns {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Agent: %s", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Output: %q", truncateForLog(tt.output, 100))
            t.Logf("Expected match: %v", tt.shouldMatch)
            
            match, pattern := detector.CheckOutput(tt.agentType, tt.output)
            
            t.Logf("Result: match=%v, pattern=%q", match, pattern)
            
            if tt.shouldMatch {
                assert.True(t, match, "Should detect rate limit")
                assert.NotEmpty(t, pattern, "Should identify pattern type")
                if tt.matchType != "" {
                    assert.Contains(t, pattern, tt.matchType, "Pattern type mismatch")
                }
            } else {
                assert.False(t, match, "Should NOT detect rate limit")
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_CapturePane(t *testing.T) {
    // Create mock tmux client
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        captureResponses: map[string]string{
            "cc_agents_1:1.0": "Normal agent output here",
            "cc_agents_1:1.1": "Error: rate limit exceeded",
            "cc_agents_1:1.2": "", // Empty pane
        },
    }
    
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        paneID      string
        expectLimit bool
    }{
        {"cc_agents_1:1.0", false},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.1", true},
        {"cc_agents_1:1.2", false},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.paneID, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Capturing pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            output, err := detector.CaptureAndCheck(tt.paneID, "cc")
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("Captured output length: %d", len(output))
            t.Logf("Limit detected: %v", tt.expectLimit)
            
            match, _ := detector.CheckOutput("cc", output)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectLimit, match)
        })
    }
}

func TestLimitDetector_BackgroundMonitoring(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{}
    detector := NewLimitDetector(testLogger(t))
    detector.tmux = mockTmux
    detector.pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    t.Log("Starting background monitoring")
    
    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 200*time.Millisecond)
    defer cancel()
    
    eventCh := detector.Events()
    
    // Start monitoring
    go detector.StartMonitoring(ctx, []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc"},
    }, "test_session")
    
    // Inject rate limit after short delay
    go func() {
        time.Sleep(75 * time.Millisecond)
        mockTmux.SetCaptureResponse("test_session:1.1", "rate limit exceeded")
    }()
    
    // Wait for event
    select {
    case event := <-eventCh:
        t.Logf("Event received: %+v", event)
        assert.Equal(t, "cc", event.AgentType)
        assert.Contains(t, event.Pattern, "rate")
    case <-ctx.Done():
        t.Log("Context cancelled before event (expected if timing varies)")
    }
}

// Helpers
func truncateForLog(s string, max int) string {
    if len(s) <= max {
        return s
    }
    return s[:max] + "..."
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log each test case input (agent type, output snippet)
- Log expected vs actual match results
- Log pattern type when matched
- Log pane capture details
- Log background monitoring events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test all known Claude Code limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Codex limit patterns
- [ ] Test all known Gemini limit patterns
- [ ] Test case insensitivity
- [ ] Test multiline output
- [ ] Test empty output
- [ ] Test partial/false matches
- [ ] Test pane capture integration
- [ ] Test background monitoring
- [ ] Mock tmux client for isolation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:54.929888923Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608090689Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:57:04.608007314Z","close_reason":"Added LimitDetector tests (all patterns, multiline, capture, monitoring); go test ./internal/swarm -run LimitDetector","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t881","depends_on_id":"bd-3un3t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1t8p","title":"EPIC: Local LLM Support via Ollama and llama.cpp","description":"## Overview
Add support for local LLM backends (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio) as first-class agent types, enabling privacy-sensitive work, offline operation, and cost-free experimentation.

## Problem Statement
Current NTM only supports cloud LLM providers (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini). Users need:
- Privacy for sensitive codebases (air-gapped environments)
- Offline operation when internet unavailable
- Cost-free experimentation and learning
- Running specialized/fine-tuned local models

## Key Features
1. New agent type: --local=N or --ollama=N for spawning local agents
2. Model selection: --local-model=codellama:34b
3. Mixed fleets: combine cloud and local agents in same session
4. Performance monitoring: track token/s, memory usage
5. Automatic model management: pull models on demand

## Technical Design
1. Ollama adapter:
   - Connect to local Ollama server (default: localhost:11434)
   - Map NTM commands to Ollama API
   - Handle streaming responses

2. llama.cpp adapter:
   - Direct integration for llama.cpp server mode
   - Support GGUF model loading
   - GPU acceleration detection

3. Agent type hierarchy:
   - CloudAgent (existing): Claude, Codex, Gemini
   - LocalAgent (new): Ollama, LlamaCpp
   - All agents implement same interface

## Configuration
- NTM_OLLAMA_HOST for custom Ollama URL
- NTM_LOCAL_MODEL for default model
- Per-project config in ntm.yaml

## Success Criteria
- Can spawn local agents alongside cloud agents
- Local agents participate in normal workflows
- Performance metrics visible in dashboard
- Works fully offline (after model cached)

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/agent/local.go (new - local agent implementation)
- internal/agent/ollama.go (new - Ollama adapter)
- internal/cli/spawn.go (add --local flags)
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/agents.go (show local agent metrics)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:29:25.999766320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:48:22.570133773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1tb8","title":"Task: Add automatic model management for local LLMs","description":"## Overview
Enable NTM to automatically pull and manage local LLM models when needed.

## Deliverables
1. Model management commands:
   - ntm models list - show available local models
   - ntm models pull <model> - download model from Ollama registry
   - ntm models remove <model> - delete model to free space
   - ntm models recommend - suggest models based on hardware

2. Automatic pull on spawn:
   - If --local-model not found locally, offer to pull
   - Show download progress in terminal
   - Continue spawn after model ready

3. Model recommendations:
   Based on available VRAM:
   - <4GB: codellama:7b-instruct, deepseek-coder:6.7b
   - 4-8GB: codellama:13b, deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct
   - 8-16GB: codellama:34b, deepseek-coder:33b
   - 16GB+: mixtral:8x7b, codellama:70b

4. Storage management:
   - Show model sizes and disk usage
   - Warn when disk space low
   - LRU cleanup suggestion for unused models

## Implementation Notes
- Use Ollama API for all model operations
- Cache model metadata locally for fast lookup
- Support offline operation with cached models

## Acceptance Criteria
- Model commands work correctly
- Automatic pull flows smoothly
- Recommendations match hardware
- Disk space warnings appear appropriately
- Unit tests for model management","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:23.066483753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:30:45.145324634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-1t8p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tb8","depends_on_id":"bd-zcft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1tml8","title":"Unit tests for internal/cli pure helpers","description":"# CLI Package Pure Function Tests

## Current State
- Coverage: 27.8%
- Package size: ~50 files, largest in codebase
- Test files: 60+ existing test files

## Target Functions (0% coverage, pure/testable)

### health.go
- [x] `statusSeverity(health.Status) int` - Maps status to numeric severity
- [x] `truncateString(s string, maxLen int) string` - Unicode-safe truncation
- [x] `coerceHealthOutput(result any) (HealthOutput, error)` - Type coercion
- Committed: 81da6aed

### workflows.go
- [x] `coordinationIcon(coord workflow.CoordinationType) string` - Icon mapping
- [x] `formatTrigger(tr workflow.Trigger) string` - Trigger formatting
- Committed: 557a9386

### swarm.go
- [x] `globToRegex(glob string) string` - Glob to regex conversion
- [x] `buildSwarmPlanOutput(plan *SwarmPlan, dryRun bool) SwarmPlanOutput` - Output builder
- Committed: 9b498a94

### Remaining opportunities (activity.go)
- [x] `stateIcon(state string) string` - Already tested
- [x] `formatActivityDuration(d time.Duration) string` - Already tested
- [x] `passesFilter(agentType string, pane tmux.Pane, opts activityOptions) bool` - Already tested

### Other files to audit
- util.go: `ExplainIfInferred`, `GetProjectRoot` (I/O-heavy)
- metrics_cmd.go: `exportCSV` (I/O), helpers
- palette.go: `loadPaletteCommands` (file I/O)
- quota.go: `runQuota` (I/O)

## Implementation Notes
- Always use `t.Parallel()` for table-driven tests
- Check existing test names in all *_test.go files before creating
- Run `go test -v ./internal/cli/... -run TestName` to verify

## Progress
- 2026-02-05: health, workflows, swarm helpers tested (81da6aed, 557a9386, 9b498a94)
- Coverage: 27.8% → 27.9% (incremental due to package size)

## Dependencies

 {"id":"bd-1tmw5","title":"CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command","description":"Add robot mode command to manually trigger account switch.

## Command Specification

```bash
ntm --robot-switch-account claude         # Switch to next Claude account
ntm --robot-switch-account openai account3  # Switch to specific account
ntm --robot-switch-account --pane agent-1 claude  # Switch for specific pane
```

## Output Schema (JSON)

```json
{
  "switch": {
    "success": true,
    "provider": "claude",
    "previous_account": "account1@email.com",
    "new_account": "account2@email.com",
    "panes_affected": ["agent-1", "agent-2"],
    "cooldown_seconds": 300
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Parse provider and optional account name
2. Call CAAM switch
3. Update affected pane environments
4. Return result JSON

## Code Location
- internal/robot/switch_account.go
- Update: internal/robot/registry.go

## Testing
- Switch to next account, verify output
- Switch to specific account
- Switch with pane filter
- Switch when no accounts available","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:52:03.353964001Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289374984Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:22:05.289301996Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-switch-account command for CAAM account switching. Added PrintSwitchAccount in robot package, flag registration in cli/root.go, and unit tests. Supports switching by provider or provider:account with optional pane filter.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-10qa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-1yty1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-26185","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tmw5","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-75iqr","title":"Task: Implement --robot-ensemble-spawn for creating ensembles","description":"Implement --robot-ensemble-spawn for creating ensembles.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION \
  --preset=architecture-review \
  --question='Review auth system' \
  --agents=cc=3,cod=2

# Explicit custom modes (advanced requires opt-in)
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION \
  --modes=A1,B13,F43 \
  --allow-advanced=true \
  --question='...'
```

### Modifiers (unprefixed)
- `--preset` or `--modes` (one required)
- `--question` (required)
- `--agents` (cc/cod/gmi counts)
- `--assignment` (round-robin|affinity|explicit)
- `--allow-advanced`
- `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`
- `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`
- `--project` (override working dir)

- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per robot-api-design.md.
- Accept mode IDs or codes in `--modes` (normalize internally).

## Response Schema
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "action": "ensemble_spawn",
  "session": "arch-review-2024-01-15",
  "preset": "architecture-review",
  "question": "Review authentication system architecture",
  "modes": [
    {"id":"deductive","code":"A1","tier":"core","pane":"..."},
    {"id":"abductive","code":"B13","tier":"advanced","pane":"..."}
  ],
  "budget": {"total_tokens": 30000},
  "status": "spawning"
}
```

## Validation Rules
- Reject advanced/experimental modes unless `allow_advanced=true`
- Reject unknown mode ids/codes
- Enforce min/max mode count
- Error codes use standard robot codes where possible:
  - `MISSING_REQUIRED` (question or preset/modes)
  - `INVALID_FLAG` (preset + modes together, incompatible flags)
  - `INVALID_INPUT` (unknown mode id/code, invalid agents)

## File Location
internal/robot/ensemble_spawn.go

## Dependencies
- EnsembleManager (bd-lp5h4)
- Validation (bd-21ygt)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Spawns ensemble with preset or explicit modes
- [ ] Honors allow_advanced gating
- [ ] RobotResponse envelope compliance
- [ ] Clear error codes for invalid inputs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:54.697779752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:43:15.965813817Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T20:43:15.965793990Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-75iqr","depends_on_id":"bd-21ygt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-75iqr","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-765vk","title":"Bug: truncate() should handle maxLen <= 0","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:18:59.348464928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:19:10.819397696Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:19:10.819333136Z","close_reason":"Fixed truncate() edge cases + added unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-77bxi","title":"TOON: Research Go integration approach (CGO vs subprocess)","description":"Evaluate approaches for calling toon_rust from Go code.

## Options

### Option A: Subprocess (Simpler)
Call toon CLI as subprocess:
```go
cmd := exec.Command("toon", "encode")
cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(jsonData)
toonOutput, _ := cmd.Output()
```

Pros: Simple, no CGO, easy updates
Cons: Process overhead per call, latency

### Option B: CGO + Rust FFI (Faster)
Build toon_rust as C-compatible library, call from Go via CGO.

Pros: Near-zero overhead, native speed
Cons: CGO complexity, cross-platform builds, version coupling

### Option C: WASM (Middle Ground)
Compile toon_rust to WASM, run via Go WASM runtime.

Pros: Sandboxed, cross-platform, single binary
Cons: WASM overhead, runtime dependency

## Recommendation

Start with **subprocess** for simplicity. If latency becomes issue, migrate to CGO.

Robot mode commands are not high-frequency (maybe 1-10/second), so subprocess overhead (~5ms) is acceptable.

## Research Tasks
1. Verify toon CLI exists and has encode/decode commands
2. Benchmark subprocess overhead
3. Evaluate if CGO is needed for any use case
4. Check if toon_rust has WASM build

## Deliverable

 {"id":"bd-7gyss","title":"Task: Ensure common flags consistency across all ntm assign commands","description":"## Overview
Ensure all ntm assign-related commands have consistent common flags.

## Common Flags to Implement
Every ntm command should support:
```bash
--json           # Machine-readable JSON output
-v, --verbose    # Show detailed scoring/decision logs
-q, --quiet      # Suppress non-essential output
--timeout=30s    # Timeout for external calls (bv, br, Agent Mail)
--no-color       # Disable colored output
```

## Agent Type Filter Consistency
Support both styles for agent type filtering:
```bash
# Short form (existing)
--cc-only
--cod-only
--gmi-only

# Long form (new, consistent with --agent in status)
--agent=claude
--agent=codex
--agent=gemini
```

## Commands to Update
- [ ] ntm assign (base command)
- [ ] ntm status --assignments (already has --agent)
- [ ] ntm assign --watch
- [ ] ntm assign --retry
- [ ] ntm assign --reassign
- [ ] ntm assign --clear
- [ ] ntm assign --pane
- [ ] ntm spawn --assign

## Implementation
```go
type CommonFlags struct {
    JSON      bool
    Verbose   int     // -v = 1, -vv = 2
    Quiet     bool
    Timeout   time.Duration
    NoColor   bool
}

func AddCommonFlags(cmd *cobra.Command, flags *CommonFlags) {
    cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&flags.JSON, "json", false, "Output as JSON")
    cmd.Flags().CountVarP(&flags.Verbose, "verbose", "v", "Increase verbosity")
    cmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&flags.Quiet, "quiet", "q", false, "Quiet mode")
    cmd.Flags().DurationVar(&flags.Timeout, "timeout", 30*time.Second, "Command timeout")
    cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&flags.NoColor, "no-color", false, "Disable colors")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All assign-related commands have common flags
- [ ] Both --cc-only and --agent=claude work
- [ ] --json output is consistent format across commands
- [ ] --verbose increases log detail
- [ ] --quiet suppresses non-error output
- [ ] --timeout affects external calls

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Common flag parsing
- Unit test: Agent filter style equivalence
- Unit test: JSON output format validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T22:03:28.850092329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:02:52.314194891Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:02:52.314054700Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7gyss","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-7ijsy","title":"xf: Implement --robot-xf-search command","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-xf-search` command for searching X/Twitter archives via xf integration.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the **harmonized** robot mode patterns for tool bridges:
- **Naming**: `--robot-xf-search` (tool prefix: xf, no inline value)
- **Query param**: `--query=QUERY` (standard modifier flag)
- **Shared modifiers**: `--limit=N`, `--since=DATE`, `--until=DATE`, `--offset=N` (global flags)
- **Tool-specific modifiers**: `--xf-mode=`, `--xf-sort=` (unique to xf)

**NOTE**: This supersedes bd-1osrc which proposed a different interface. This bead is the canonical implementation.

## Command Interface

```bash
# Basic search (uses --query= modifier, NOT inline value)
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="error handling patterns"

# With search mode
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust async" --xf-mode=semantic --limit=10

# With date range filter
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="claude code" --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-06-30

# With pagination
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="ai agents" --limit=20 --offset=20

# Sort by date (newest first)
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="llm" --xf-sort=date
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "query": "error handling patterns",
  "mode": "hybrid",
  "sort": "relevance",
  "count": 5,
  "offset": 0,
  "hits": [
    {
      "type": "tweet",
      "date": "2023-06-15",
      "content": "Been thinking about error handling patterns in Rust...",
      "score": 0.95,
      "url": "https://x.com/user/status/123456",
      "author": "rustacean",
      "thread_id": "123456"
    }
  ],
  "total_matches": 150,
  "has_more": true,
  "_agent_hints": {
    "next_offset": 5,
    "pages_remaining": 29,
    "suggested_query_refinement": null
  }
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "error": "xf not found in PATH",
  "error_code": "TOOL_NOT_FOUND",
  "hint": "Install xf: go install github.com/.../xf@latest"
}
```

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default | Scope |
|------|-------------|---------|-------|
| `--query=Q` | Search query (required) | - | **Required** |
| `--xf-mode=M` | Search mode: keyword, semantic, hybrid | hybrid | Tool-specific |
| `--xf-sort=S` | Sort order: relevance, date, date-asc | relevance | Tool-specific |
| `--limit=N` | Max results to return | 20 | Global shared |
| `--offset=N` | Skip first N results (pagination) | 0 | Global shared |
| `--since=DATE` | Filter to tweets after date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |
| `--until=DATE` | Filter to tweets before date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |

## Code Location
- internal/robot/xf_search.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-xf-search` flag registered in root.go (bool flag)
- [ ] `--query=` required modifier for search term
- [ ] `--xf-mode=` accepts: keyword, semantic, hybrid (default: hybrid)
- [ ] `--xf-sort=` accepts: relevance, date, date-asc (default: relevance)
- [ ] `--limit=N` caps results (default 20) - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--offset=N` skips first N results for pagination - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--since=DATE` filters by start date - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--until=DATE` filters by end date - uses global shared flag
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly (includes timestamp, offset, total_matches)
- [ ] Missing xf returns actionable error with hint and error_code
- [ ] Empty results returns success with empty hits array
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on error, 2 if xf unavailable
- [ ] `_agent_hints` includes `next_offset` for pagination guidance

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/xf_search_test.go)

| Test Case | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `TestXFSearchFlagParsing` | Parse --query, --xf-mode, --xf-sort, --limit, --offset, --since, --until |
| `TestXFSearchQueryRequired` | Error when --query is missing |
| `TestXFSearchModeValidation` | Accept keyword/semantic/hybrid, reject invalid modes |
| `TestXFSearchSortValidation` | Accept relevance/date/date-asc, reject invalid sorts |
| `TestXFSearchDateParsing` | Parse --since and --until dates in YYYY-MM-DD format |
| `TestXFSearchDateValidation` | Reject invalid date formats |
| `TestXFSearchDateRangeValidation` | Error if --until is before --since |
| `TestXFSearchOffsetValidation` | Accept non-negative integers, reject negative |
| `TestXFSearchOutputParsing` | Parse xf JSON output correctly |
| `TestXFSearchScoreNormalization` | Normalize scores to 0.0-1.0 range |
| `TestXFSearchJSONSerialization` | Output matches schema with all required fields |
| `TestXFSearchTimestampIncluded` | Response includes RFC3339 timestamp |
| `TestXFSearchMissingXF` | Error with TOOL_NOT_FOUND when xf not in PATH |
| `TestXFSearchXFFailure` | Handle xf command failures gracefully |
| `TestXFSearchEmptyResults` | Return success with empty hits array |
| `TestXFSearchExitCodes` | Map errors to correct exit codes (0/1/2) |
| `TestXFSearchLimitEnforced` | Limit caps results correctly |
| `TestXFSearchOffsetApplied` | Offset skips correct number of results |
| `TestXFSearchHasMoreFlag` | Set has_more when total_matches > offset + count |
| `TestXFSearchNextOffsetHint` | Calculate next_offset correctly in _agent_hints |
| `TestXFSearchPagesRemaining` | Calculate pages_remaining correctly |
| `TestXFSearchThreadIdIncluded` | Include thread_id in hit objects |

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_xf.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# [E2E-XF] Robot XF Search End-to-End Tests
# =============================================================================
# Tests the --robot-xf-search command for X/Twitter archive integration.
# Exit codes: 0=all passed, 1=test failed, 77=skipped (xf not installed)
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-XF]"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
SKIPPED=0

log_info()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [INFO]  $*"; }
log_pass()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [PASS]  $*"; ((PASSED++)); }
log_fail()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [FAIL]  $*"; ((FAILED++)); }
log_skip()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [SKIP]  $*"; ((SKIPPED++)); }
log_debug() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [DEBUG] $*"; }

cleanup() {
    log_info "Test summary: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_info "Starting robot xf E2E tests"
log_info "Test timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prerequisite Check
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

if ! command -v xf &>/dev/null; then
    log_skip "xf not installed - skipping all tests"
    exit 77
fi

XF_VERSION=$(xf --version 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
log_info "xf version: $XF_VERSION"

if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
    log_fail "jq is required for tests"
    exit 1
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-001: Basic search with --query
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-001: Basic search with --query"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test query" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.query == "test query"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("timestamp") and has("total_matches") and has("offset")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "XF-001: Basic search works with all required fields"
        else
            log_fail "XF-001: Missing required fields (timestamp, total_matches, or offset)"
        fi
    else
        log_fail "XF-001: Query not echoed in response"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-001: Search did not succeed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-002: Search with --xf-mode=semantic
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-002: Search with --xf-mode=semantic"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust async" --xf-mode=semantic 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.mode == "semantic"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-002: Semantic mode accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-002: Mode not set to semantic"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-003: Search with --xf-mode=keyword
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-003: Search with --xf-mode=keyword"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust" --xf-mode=keyword 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.mode == "keyword"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-003: Keyword mode accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-003: Mode not set to keyword"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-004: Search with --limit
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-004: Search with --limit=5"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="programming" --limit=5 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

HITS_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.hits | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
if [[ "$HITS_COUNT" -le 5 ]]; then
    log_pass "XF-004: Limit respected (got $HITS_COUNT hits)"
else
    log_fail "XF-004: Limit exceeded (expected <=5, got $HITS_COUNT)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-005: Search with --since date filter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-005: Search with --since=2024-01-01"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="ai" --since=2024-01-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-005: --since date filter accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-005: Search with --since failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-006: Search with --until date filter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-006: Search with --until=2024-12-31"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="ai" --until=2024-12-31 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-006: --until date filter accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-006: Search with --until failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-007: Search with date range (--since and --until)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-007: Search with date range"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="llm" --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-06-30 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-007: Date range accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-007: Date range search failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-008: Invalid --xf-mode value
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-008: Invalid --xf-mode value"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --xf-mode=invalid 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.error_code' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "XF-008: Invalid mode rejected with error_code"
    else
        log_fail "XF-008: Missing error_code in error response"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-008: Invalid mode should fail"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-009: Empty results handling
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-009: Empty results handling"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="xyznonexistentquery98765" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.hits == []' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.total_matches == 0' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "XF-009: Empty results handled correctly"
        else
            log_pass "XF-009: Empty hits array (total_matches may differ)"
        fi
    else
        log_pass "XF-009: No results handled (may have unexpected matches)"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-009: Empty results should still succeed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-010: Missing --query parameter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-010: Missing --query parameter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search 2>&1) || true
EXIT_CODE=$?
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"
log_debug "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.error' 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "query.*required"; then
        log_pass "XF-010: Missing query correctly rejected"
    else
        log_fail "XF-010: Error message should mention query is required"
    fi
elif echo "$RESULT" | grep -qi "query.*required"; then
    log_pass "XF-010: Missing query correctly rejected (stderr)"
else
    log_fail "XF-010: Should require --query"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-011: Default mode is hybrid
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-011: Default mode is hybrid"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="default mode test" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.mode == "hybrid"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-011: Default mode is hybrid"
else
    MODE=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.mode' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
    log_fail "XF-011: Expected default mode hybrid, got $MODE"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-012: Pagination with --offset
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-012: Pagination with --offset"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="code" --limit=5 --offset=10 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    OFFSET=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.offset' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    if [[ "$OFFSET" -eq 10 ]]; then
        log_pass "XF-012: Offset applied correctly"
    else
        log_fail "XF-012: Offset not reflected in response (expected 10, got $OFFSET)"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-012: Pagination failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-013: Sort by date
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-013: Sort by date"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --xf-sort=date 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    SORT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.sort' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
    if [[ "$SORT" == "date" ]]; then
        log_pass "XF-013: Sort by date accepted"
    else
        log_fail "XF-013: Sort not set to date (got $SORT)"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-013: Sort by date failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-014: Agent hints with pagination info
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-014: Agent hints with pagination info"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="programming" --limit=5 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("_agent_hints")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '._agent_hints | has("next_offset")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "XF-014: Agent hints include next_offset"
        else
            log_fail "XF-014: Agent hints missing next_offset"
        fi
    else
        log_fail "XF-014: Missing _agent_hints"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-014: Search failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-015: Combined options
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-015: Combined options"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust" --xf-mode=semantic --xf-sort=date --limit=3 --offset=5 --since=2023-06-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true and .mode == "semantic" and .sort == "date"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    HITS_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.hits | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    OFFSET=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.offset' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    if [[ "$HITS_COUNT" -le 3 ]] && [[ "$OFFSET" -eq 5 ]]; then
        log_pass "XF-015: Combined options work correctly"
    else
        log_fail "XF-015: Limit or offset not respected with combined options"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-015: Combined options failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-016: Exit code verification
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-016: Exit code verification"
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --xf-mode=invalid >/dev/null 2>&1 || EXIT_CODE=$?
if [[ "${EXIT_CODE:-0}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    log_pass "XF-016: Exit code 1 for invalid input"
else
    log_fail "XF-016: Expected exit code 1, got ${EXIT_CODE:-0}"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-017: Invalid date range (until before since)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-017: Invalid date range"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --since=2024-06-01 --until=2024-01-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-017: Invalid date range rejected"
else
    log_fail "XF-017: Should reject --until before --since"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Final Summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "========================================="
log_info "Final Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
log_info "========================================="

if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
fi

log_info "All robot xf E2E tests passed!"
exit 0
```

## API Harmonization Notes

This bead was updated to follow the canonical robot mode tool bridge pattern:
- Tool bridges use `--robot-<tool>-<action>` (bool flag)
- Parameters use modifier flags (`--query=`, `--limit=`)
- Global shared flags (`--limit`, `--since`, `--until`, `--offset`) are NOT tool-prefixed
- Tool-specific flags (`--xf-mode`, `--xf-sort`) ARE tool-prefixed
